alkylation reagent: alkylation of free thiols was performed using iodoacetamide
alkylation concentration: using iodoacetamide (46.6 mg/mL in 25 mM Ambic, 5 μL, 30 min in the dark)
cleavage agent: The samples were digested with trypsin
fragmentation method: selected for high energy collisional dissociation (HCD, 30,000 fwhm resolution, AGC 1e5, MFT 300 ms)
instrument: coupled to a hybrid linear quadrupole/orbitrap mass spectrometer (Q Exactive HF Quadrupole-Orbitrap, Thermo Scientific)
ionization type: equipped with a nanospray ionization source
reduction reagent: cysteine reduction was then performed by the addition of dithiothreitol (DTT, 11.1 mg/mL in 25 mM AmBic, 5 μL)
reduction concentration: DTT, 11.1 mg/mL in 25 mM AmBic, 5 μL
